Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LUIS MAGALLON'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET TWO

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Luis Magallon's motion to compel further responses to his second set of special interrogatories from plaintiff Sarah Lewis, and for sanctions, is granted in part. The court orders further responses as set out below and orders Lewis to pay $500 to Magallon in partial sanctions within 30 days.

Lewis answered the discovery at issue on this motion on March 9, 2026. Her responses were inadequate and contained objections to valid discovery requests. Her responses did not provide any concrete information. Magallon's meet and confer efforts came late; his meet and confer letter was sent on April 13, 2026. (Desai Decl., para. 7.) Lewis responded by email on April 13 saying that she was willing to supplement as more information became available. (Lewis Decl., para. 5.) Lewis did not offer to amend and does not aver that she addressed the point-by-point issues identified in Magallon's letter. (Desai Decl., Ex. D.) Magallon filed this motion on April 23, 2026. Lewis filed a late opposition on May 12, 2026. With her opposition, she attached verified supplemental responses that were also dated May 12. (Lewis Decl., Ex. 1.)

The court exercises its discretion to consider the late opposition. Because the hearing date on the motion was moved, Magallon suffers no prejudice and could have filed a reply.

The motion is not moot, notwithstanding Lewis's late amendment. Because Lewis's initial objections were patently without merit, and because she offered only a vague promise to supplement later rather than addressing the legitimate and concrete complaints raised in Magallon's meet and confer letter, the court concludes that Magallon adequately met and conferred, and the court finds that Lewis would not have amended had Magallon not filed this motion. The court bases its order partly on its considerable experience with discovery disputes in this case.

Lewis's responses remain deficient and address some but not all of the issues raised in Magallon's letter. Because trial is fast approaching and Magallon is entitled to more definite answers, the court addresses certain deficiencies here.

Nos. 26, 29: If Magallon contends the response is inadequate, he may meet and confer with Lewis.

Nos. 27, 28, 30: Magallon seeks information about damages in total and damages attributable to separate defendants. Lewis provides a concrete response regarding rent she has covered but provides an insufficient response as to (1) past medical bills she contends are related to defendants' conduct; (2) past income loss Lewis contends she has suffered; and (3) out of pocket costs. Even if Lewis does not have complete information, she must provide what information she has at this time. These are concrete, already-incurred, calculable amounts. Lewis shall amend with all of the specific damage amounts available to her at this time.

No. 39: Magallon seeks a statement of all past wage loss suffered by Lewis. Lewis describes her wage loss in general terms but does not state any amounts. Magallon is entitled to specific information about the amount of damages Lewis claims she has suffered. Lewis shall amend.

The further responses ordered herein shall be served within 10 days.

The court concludes that Lewis's discovery position lacks substantial justification. However, the court reduces the sanction to account for Magallon's failure to initiate meet and confer efforts earlier. Lewis shall pay $500 to Magallon within 30 days.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. Mr. Smolar also contests the court's tentative ruling in that it grants plaintiff leave to amend. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ENA REALES AND LUIS MAGALLON'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED as follows: Defendant's request to strike specified general damages is GRANTED. Under CCP Section 425.10(a)(2), an amount demanded shall not be stated in an action for personal injury, so the language "of $250,000" in Plaintiff's prayer for relief is hereby stricken. Defendant's request to strike punitive damages is GRANTED with leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to state sufficient facts as to Defendant Reales and Magallon to support punitive damages. Plaintiff shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to amend.
